note: Mathlib/Order/SetNotation.lean:44 -- class SupSet (α : Type*) where sSup : Set α → α, the supremum operation (join of a set) in a preorder; the general formalization of 'supremum' underlying ConditionallyCompleteLattice/CompleteLattice.
verify_note: Line 44 confirmed verbatim (class SupSet with sSup : Set a -> a); docstring 'Class for the sSup operator' / 'Supremum of a set' -- this operation typeclass genuinely is Mathlib's formalization of the sup operation, consistent with how Add/Mul-style operation classes are treated as exact elsewhere in this batch.

note: Mathlib/Order/SetNotation.lean:50 -- class InfSet (α : Type*) where sInf : Set α → α, the infimum operation (meet of a set) in a preorder; the general formalization of 'infimum', paired with SupSet for the same QID.
verify_note: Line 50 confirmed verbatim (class InfSet with sInf : Set a -> a), paired dual of SupSet with matching docstring 'Infimum of a set'; exact is defensible on the same grounds as SupSet.

note: Mathlib/Computability/DFA.lean:58 -- structure DFA (α σ) with step/start/accept fields; docstring 'A Deterministic Finite Automaton (DFA) is a state machine which decides membership in a particular Language'. Formalizes the deterministic variant of a finite-state machine, not the fully general notion (no output/Mealy-Moore machines), so 'related' rather than 'exact'.
verify_note: Line 58 confirmed (structure DFA with step/start/accept); docstring literally 'A Deterministic Finite Automaton (DFA) is a state machine which decides membership in a particular Language'. Wikidata Q176452 description is the general FSM notion (can include Mealy/Moore output machines); DFA is the acceptor-only restriction, so 'related' rather than 'exact' is the honest call, not an overclaim.

note: Mathlib/Order/Heyting/Basic.lean:155 -- class HeytingAlgebra; file docstring explicitly states 'Heyting algebras precisely model intuitionistic logic, whereas Boolean algebras model classical logic.' This is the algebraic-semantics model, not a formalized proof calculus for intuitionistic logic itself, hence 'related'.
verify_note: Line 155 confirmed (class HeytingAlgebra extends GeneralizedHeytingAlgebra, OrderBot, Compl); file docstring quote verified verbatim at line 24: 'Heyting algebras precisely model intuitionistic logic, whereas Boolean algebras model classical logic.' This is algebraic semantics for the logic, not the proof calculus itself, so 'related' (not 'exact') is correctly calibrated.

note: Mathlib/Data/Nat/Choose/Basic.lean:63 -- theorem choose_succ_succ (n k) : choose (succ n) (succ k) = choose n k + choose n (succ k); this is Pascal's rule, the defining recurrence of Pascal's triangle (Nat.choose values are literally the triangle's entries; see also 'sum of entries in a row of Pascal's triangle' docstrings in Choose/Sum.lean).
verify_note: Line 63 confirmed verbatim (choose (succ n) (succ k) = choose n k + choose n (succ k), proved rfl -- it mirrors Nat.choose's own recursive definition). Wikidata Q177051 description is 'triangular array ... each entry is the sum of the two entries above it', which this recurrence states precisely; defensible as exact under this batch's convention of matching an operation/property's defining fact rather than requiring a like-named decl.

note: verified via decl-name oracle (docLink ./Init/Core.html#Std.Associative, kind class) -- core Lean/Std class marking a binary operation op as associative (op (op a b) c = op a (op b c)); Mathlib's own mul_assoc/add_assoc (Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Defs.html#mul_assoc) instantiate it per-structure. This class IS the abstract formalization of the associative property.
verify_note: Verified in Lean core Init/Core.lean (namespace Std, ~line 2479): class Associative (op) : Prop with assoc : (a b c) -> op (op a b) c = op a (op b c) -- exactly the textbook associative property, matching Wikidata Q177251 description 'property of binary operations allowing sequences of operations to be regrouped'. Spot-checked supporting claim (mul_assoc at Mathlib/Algebra/Group/Defs.lean:193) is also real.

note: Verified 'Projectivization' is a key under libraries.Mathlib.modules.LinearAlgebra.sub in catalog/data/hierarchy.json. Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Projectivization/Basic.lean:14 -- 'This file contains the definition of the projectivization of a vector space over a field, as well as the bijection between said projectivization and the collection of all one dimensional subspaces', plus Subspace.lean/Collinear.lean/Independence.lean/Action.lean/PSL/* build out points, lines, collinearity and projective transformations -- this folder is projective geometry's formal home in Mathlib.
verify_note: Confirmed 'Projectivization' is a key at libraries.Mathlib.modules.LinearAlgebra.sub in catalog/data/hierarchy.json (110 decls, submodules Constructions/Basic/Action/Independence/Subspace/Cardinality); folder on disk also has Collinear.lean and PSL/{Stabilizer,PSL2}.lean as the note describes.
